# QUIC/H3 Long-Haul Soak and Adversarial Network Simulations

**Bead**: `asupersync-2oh2u.4.7` ([T4.7])
**Generated by**: SapphireHill (claude-code / opus-4.6)
**Date**: 2026-03-03
**Dependencies**: T4.6 (interoperability matrix)
**Purpose**: Define deterministic soak and adversarial network simulation scenarios for the asupersync native QUIC/H3 stack. Validates stability, cancellation correctness, and performance consistency under sustained load, loss, jitter, partition, and path-change conditions.

---

## 1. Scope

This document defines simulation scenarios that exercise the native QUIC connection machine and HTTP/3 protocol layer under adverse conditions. All scenarios are deterministic (using `DetRng` and `DetClock`) and require no async runtime or network I/O.

### 1.1 Simulation Categories

| Category | ID Prefix | Count | Focus |
|---|---|---|---|
| Sustained load (soak) | SOAK-* | 5 | Long-running stream creation/teardown under steady state |
| Packet loss | LOSS-* | 4 | Random and burst loss patterns with recovery validation |
| Jitter/reorder | JITTER-* | 3 | Out-of-order delivery and variable latency |
| Partition/heal | PART-* | 3 | Complete connectivity loss followed by recovery |
| Adversarial input | ADV-* | 5 | Malformed frames, resource exhaustion, protocol violations |
| Cancellation stress | CANCEL-* | 4 | Cx-based cancellation at various lifecycle points |
| **Total** | | **24** | |

### 1.2 Invariants Under Test

| Invariant | ID | Description |
|---|---|---|
| Connection stability | INV-1 | Connection must remain Established through transient faults |
| Stream cleanup | INV-2 | All opened streams must reach a terminal state (closed, reset, or drained) |
| Flow control consistency | INV-3 | `bytes_in_flight` must decrease monotonically as ACKs arrive |
| Memory bounds | INV-4 | Active stream count must never exceed configured MAX_STREAMS |
| Drain completeness | INV-5 | After close, connection must reach Closed within drain_timeout |
| Cancellation safety | INV-6 | Cx cancellation must not corrupt connection state |
| ACK convergence | INV-7 | All ack-eliciting packets must eventually be ACKed or declared lost |
| Transport metrics | INV-8 | Counters (sent, acked, lost, retransmitted) must be self-consistent |

---

## 2. Scenario Definitions

### 2.1 Soak Scenarios (SOAK-*)

| ID | Description | Duration | Streams | Loss Rate | Assertions |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| SOAK-1 | Steady stream creation/teardown (no loss) | 1000 rounds | 100 bidi | 0% | INV-1, INV-2, INV-3, INV-8 |
| SOAK-2 | High-volume unidirectional streams | 500 rounds | 200 uni | 0% | INV-1, INV-2, INV-4 |
| SOAK-3 | Mixed bidi + uni with background loss | 500 rounds | 150 mixed | 5% | INV-1, INV-3, INV-7, INV-8 |
| SOAK-4 | Window exhaustion and recovery cycles | 200 rounds | 50 bidi | 0% | INV-3, INV-8 |
| SOAK-5 | Rapid stream open/close churn | 1000 rounds | 500 bidi (short) | 0% | INV-2, INV-4 |

### 2.2 Loss Scenarios (LOSS-*)

| ID | Description | Pattern | Loss Rate | Assertions |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| LOSS-1 | Uniform random loss | Each packet dropped independently | 10% | INV-3, INV-7, INV-8 |
| LOSS-2 | Burst loss (Gilbert model) | 3-5 consecutive packets dropped | ~15% avg | INV-1, INV-7, INV-8 |
| LOSS-3 | Tail loss (last N packets dropped) | Last 3 packets of burst | Varies | INV-7, INV-8 |
| LOSS-4 | ACK loss (return path) | ACK packets dropped | 20% | INV-1, INV-3, INV-8 |

### 2.3 Jitter/Reorder Scenarios (JITTER-*)

| ID | Description | Parameter | Assertions |
|---|---|---|---|
| JITTER-1 | Random RTT jitter | RTT +/- 50% variance | INV-1, INV-3 |
| JITTER-2 | Packet reordering | Swap adjacent packets | INV-1, INV-7 |
| JITTER-3 | Progressive latency increase | RTT doubles every 50 packets | INV-1, INV-5 |

### 2.4 Partition/Heal Scenarios (PART-*)

| ID | Description | Duration | Assertions |
|---|---|---|---|
| PART-1 | Short partition (< idle timeout) | 5 seconds | INV-1 (connection survives) |
| PART-2 | Long partition (> idle timeout) | 120 seconds | INV-5 (connection closes) |
| PART-3 | Flapping connectivity | 3 on/off cycles | INV-1, INV-2 |

### 2.5 Adversarial Input Scenarios (ADV-*)

| ID | Description | Input | Expected Behavior |
|---|---|---|---|
| ADV-1 | Oversized stream data | Single frame > flow window | Stream reset with FLOW_CONTROL_ERROR |
| ADV-2 | Invalid stream ID parity | Client opens server-initiated stream | STREAM_STATE_ERROR |
| ADV-3 | Duplicate H3 SETTINGS | Control stream sends SETTINGS twice | H3 protocol error |
| ADV-4 | Zero-length connection ID overflow | CID > 20 bytes | Connection close |
| ADV-5 | Rapid stream reset flood | 100 resets in 1ms | INV-4 (bounded resources) |

### 2.6 Cancellation Stress Scenarios (CANCEL-*)

| ID | Description | Cancel Point | Assertions |
|---|---|---|---|
| CANCEL-1 | Cancel during stream send | Mid-write | INV-6, INV-2 |
| CANCEL-2 | Cancel during handshake | After begin_handshake, before 1rtt | INV-5, INV-6 |
| CANCEL-3 | Cancel with pending ACKs | While ACK processing | INV-6, INV-8 |
| CANCEL-4 | Double cancel (idempotent) | Cancel after already cancelled | INV-6 |

---

## 3. Simulation Infrastructure

### 3.1 Network Fault Injector

```
NetworkFaultInjector {
    rng: DetRng,
    loss_rate: f64,
    burst_length: Range<u64>,
    reorder_window: u64,
    jitter_micros: Range<u64>,
    partition_schedule: Vec<(u64, u64)>,  // (start, end) pairs in microseconds
}
```

### 3.2 Metrics Collector

```
SoakMetrics {
    packets_sent: u64,
    packets_acked: u64,
    packets_lost: u64,
    bytes_in_flight_peak: u64,
    streams_opened: u64,
    streams_closed: u64,
    round_trips: u64,
    max_rtt_micros: u64,
    min_rtt_micros: u64,
}
```

### 3.3 Determinism Guarantee

All scenarios use:
- `DetRng::new(seed)` for reproducible random choices
- `DetClock` for microsecond-precision virtual time
- No `tokio::time`, no `std::time::Instant`, no system clock dependency
- Fixed seed per test for regression reproducibility

---

## 4. Pass/Fail Criteria

| Criterion | Threshold | Measurement |
|---|---|---|
| Connection survival (soak) | 100% rounds complete without error | `connection.state() == Established` after each round |
| Stream cleanup completeness | All opened streams must be cleaned up | `streams_opened == streams_closed + streams_reset` |
| Flow control invariant | `bytes_in_flight` monotonically decreases as ACKs arrive | Check after each ACK batch |
| ACK convergence | 100% of sent packets must be ACKed or declared lost | `packets_sent == packets_acked + packets_lost` at end |
| Cancel safety | Connection state must not be corrupted post-cancel | State machine transitions remain valid |
| Adversarial rejection | Invalid inputs produce specific error codes | Match expected error variant |
| Partition recovery | Connection recovers after short partition | `state() == Established` after heal |
| Drain completion | After close, reach Closed within timeout | `state() == Closed` before deadline |

---

## 5. Drift Detection

| Rule | Trigger | Action |
|---|---|---|
| DRIFT-S1 | New loss/CC mechanism added to `quic_native/transport.rs` | Add soak scenario exercising new path |
| DRIFT-S2 | Stream limit changed in `quic_native/streams.rs` | Update SOAK-2/SOAK-5 stream counts |
| DRIFT-S3 | Drain timeout logic changed | Update PART-2 duration threshold |
| DRIFT-S4 | New H3 frame type supported | Add ADV-* scenario for malformed variant |
